name: Coquito
title: BACARDÍ Coquito | Coconut Liqueur | Coconut Liqueur Drinks | BACARDÍ Global
short_description: A cream-based coconut liqueur, Coquito is a traditional Puerto Rican holiday cocktail.
marketing_description: BACARDÍ Coquito is made with BACARDÍ Superior Rum and a mix of vanilla, cinnamon and other festive spices. Looking for an authentic, delicious Caribbean cocktail this holiday season? Look no further than BACARDÍ Coquito. Coquito meaning “Little Coconut” in Spanish is a festive, Puerto Rican holiday drink that is synonymous with BACARDÍ's Caribbean roots.
tasting_notes: A silky coconut flavor, toasty spices and hints of vanilla and cinnamon that celebrate the Caribbean culture and holidays.

faq_items.question: What is the difference between dark, gold and white rum?
faq_items.answer: The color of rum helps us know what flavors it brings. Dark is sweet and smoky, gold is rich and soothing, and white is smooth and subtle.

faq_items.question: HOW DO YOU MAKE RUM?
faq_items.answer: We make rum by distilling sugarcane molasses and fermenting it with yeast and water. Rum originates from Barbados with its roots going back as far as the 17th century.

faq_items.question: Is rum sweet?
faq_items.answer: Many think that, since rum is made from sugar cane, it must be sweet. But rum comes in a range of sweetness, from barely-there to an all-out sugar rush.
